text.title: Guardrails AI — Validator-Based Output Pipeline — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `guardrails-ai-framework` must enforce. Verifiable at review time; failures gate the artifact.

rule.statement: Every Guardrails pipeline ships with a typed schema (Pydantic class or RAIL XML).
rule.rationale: Without a schema there is no contract for validators to check against.

rule.statement: Cap on-fail reask attempts at 3; budget alarm at 5 if any validator triggers >5x in 10 calls.
rule.rationale: Unbounded reask loops cost 5-10x normal inference; cap protects cost + latency budgets.

rule.statement: Every validator declares its on-fail policy explicitly (reask / fix / refrain / filter / noop).
rule.rationale: Default-on-fail behaviour differs across versions; explicit policy is auditable.

rule.statement: PII validators (DetectPII, AnonymiseText) MUST use on_fail='filter' or 'fix', never 'noop'.

rationale.source: GDPR Article 32; Guardrails Hub PII validators

rule.rationale: PII leaking past noop is a GDPR / data-residency breach.

rule.statement: Every validator failure is logged with validator_id + reason + reask_count.
rule.rationale: Without traces, repeat-fail patterns and cost spikes are invisible.

rule.statement: Pin Hub validator versions; never use floating @latest.
rule.rationale: Hub validators change behaviour across versions; floating tag introduces silent regressions.
